title: buildah pulls image in a loop if non-root user not in /etc/sub[ug]id
body:
Trying to pull an image as non-root user. buildah downloads the image, then restarts the download in never ending loop (it seems)
```
$ buildah pull balenalib/amd64-debian-python:3.8-run-20200503
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 54fec2fa59d0 done  
Copying blob 5df9ebbfbd78 done  
Copying blob 4404778e5a87 done  
Copying blob 9b92efda9ff0 done

Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 5df9ebbfbd78 done  
Copying blob 9b92efda9ff0 done  
Copying blob 4404778e5a87 done  
Copying blob dc1c1c303cbc done  
Copying blob 192f88aa1ac3 done  
Copying blob 54fec2fa59d0 [==================>-------------------] 12.8MiB / 25.8MiB
Copying blob 76b4827cb468 done  
Copying blob cf7d1743015f done  
Copying blob b32db33f1f67 done  
Copying blob 5541044649a7 done  
Copying blob b5a6ff9eb8a2 done  
Copying blob 7c2a70506402 done  
Copying blob 5e2483d52c12 done  
Copying blob 8d83ec97a1a1 done  
Copying blob c1f426d49ea9 [===========================>----------] 21.2MiB / 29.2MiB
Copying blob b75b07940382 done  
Copying blob 1a5140a7bddd done  
Copying blob a1f95650c3f9 done  
Copying blob f05c49bfdcff done  
Copying blob e78b6d97a3fc done  
^C
```

This is the version I am using
```
$ buildah --version
buildah version 1.14.9 (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.1-dev)
```
I am running `buildah pull` as non-root user. I have not configured the user with any special privileges.

I would expect buildah either to download the images and stop or to raise an error.

comments.body:
What platform?
What does `buildah info` say?
Do you have fuse-overlayfs installed?

This is ArchLinux distro.

```
$ uname -a    
Linux greymatter 5.6.15-arch1-1 #1 SMP PREEMPT Wed, 27 May 2020 23:42:26 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```
```
$ pacman -Q glibc
glibc 2.31-5
```
```
$ pacman -Q fuse-overlayfs
fuse-overlayfs 1.0.0-2
```
```
$ buildah info
{
    "host": {
        "CgroupVersion": "v1",
        "Distribution": {
            "distribution": "arch",
            "version": "unknown"
        },
        "MemTotal": 33448464384,
        "MenFree": 17242664960,
        "OCIRuntime": "runc",
        "SwapFree": 6949240832,
        "SwapTotal": 6949240832,
        "arch": "amd64",
        "cpus": 8,
        "hostname": "greymatter",
        "kernel": "5.6.15-arch1-1",
        "os": "linux",
        "rootless": true,
        "uptime": "131h 21m 50.31s (Approximately 5.46 days)"
    },
    "store": {
        "ContainerStore": {
            "number": 0
        },
        "GraphDriverName": "overlay",
        "GraphOptions": [
            "overlay.mount_program=/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
        ],
        "GraphRoot": "/home/wrobell/.local/share/containers/storage",
        "GraphStatus": {
            "Backing Filesystem": "btrfs",
            "Native Overlay Diff": "false",
            "Supports d_type": "true",
            "Using metacopy": "false"
        },
        "ImageStore": {
            "number": 0
        },
        "RunRoot": "/run/user/1000/containers"
    }
}
@giuseppe Do you think there is an issue with fuse-overlay on top of btrfs?

I get exactly the same behaviour on my Raspberry Pi, which has ext4 filesystem.

```
$ uname -a
Linux rpi-mm4 5.4.42-1-ARCH #1 SMP PREEMPT Tue May 26 01:49:01 UTC 2020 armv7l GNU/Linux
```
```
$ pacman -Q glibc
glibc 2.31-2
```
```
$ pacman -Q fuse-overlayfs
fuse-overlayfs 1.0.0-2
```
```
$ buildah info
{
    "host": {
        "CgroupVersion": "v1",
        "Distribution": {
            "distribution": "archarm",
            "version": "unknown"
        },
        "MemTotal": 4024434688,
        "MenFree": 1052610560,
        "OCIRuntime": "runc",
        "SwapFree": 0,
        "SwapTotal": 0,
        "arch": "arm",
        "cpus": 4,
        "hostname": "rpi-mm4",
        "kernel": "5.4.42-1-ARCH",
        "os": "linux",
        "rootless": true,
        "uptime": "244h 45m 48.94s (Approximately 10.17 days)"
    },
    "store": {
        "ContainerStore": {
            "number": 0
        },
        "GraphDriverName": "overlay",
        "GraphOptions": [
            "overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
        ],
        "GraphRoot": "/home/wrobell/.local/share/containers/storage",
        "GraphStatus": {
            "Backing Filesystem": "extfs",
            "Native Overlay Diff": "false",
            "Supports d_type": "true",
            "Using metacopy": "false"
        },
        "ImageStore": {
            "number": 0
        },
        "RunRoot": "/run/user/1001/containers"
    }
}
```

> @giuseppe Do you think there is an issue with fuse-overlay on top of btrfs?

I think we don't get that far.  There should not be any fuse-overlayfs mount during pulling.

Could you please run `buildah --log-level debug balenalib/amd64-debian-python:3.8-run-20200503` and attach the output?  Hopefully the debug log will have some useful information

it seems we are not printing any useful error message, while on amd64 I get:

```
* "docker.io/balenalib/amd64-debian-python:3.8-run-20200503": Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:54fec2fa59d0a0de9cd2dec9850b36c43de451f1fd1c0a5bf8f1cf26a61a5da4": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 0:42 for /etc/gshadow): lchown /etc/gshadow: invalid argument
```

Could you try adding more uids to `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid` for your user or alternatively use IgnoreChownErrors (you can do it adding `ignore_chown_errors = true` in your ~/.config/containers/storage.conf` file, under storage/storage.options)?

As I mentioned originally, I had not configured my user initially. I had no `/etc/sub[gu]id` files.

After creating `/etc/sub[gu]id` files and running `podman system migrate`, buildah downloads image and exits.

IMHO, it should fail before even starting the download.

The problem is it does not know the the UID that it can not support. If I gave a user 1000 UIDs and the image needs 1001 then this will fail.  We should be failing when the first chown happens, I agree, I do not know why the failure is not bubbling up to podman.

> The problem is it does not know the the UID that it can not support. If I gave a user 1000 UIDs and the image needs 1001 then this will fail. We should be failing when the first chown happens, I agree, I do not know why the failure is not bubbling up to podman.

it might be something platform specific.  I've tried the exact same use case and the error is propagated correctly to buildah.
